name: QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - Weak Public Key Length
notes: QRadar Network Insights has detected an SSL/TLS session which uses a certificate which has a low public key bit count. A server providing a weak Public Key Certificate (less than 1024 bits) may represent a security risk. According to NIST publication 800-57, the recommended minimum key length beginning in 2011 is 2048 bits for RSA and 256 bits for ECDSA.
conditions_text: when the flow matches Flow Direction is any of [R2L or L2R or R2R]
when the flow matches X509 Certificate Public Key Algorithm is not N/A, X509 Certificate Public Key Size is not N/A

when the flow matches ("x509 certificate public key size" < 2048 AND "x509 certificate public key algorithm" != 'id-ecPublicKey') OR ("x509 certificate public key size" < 256 AND "x509 certificate public key algorithm" = 'id-ecPublicKey') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - Weak Public Key Length on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the flow matches Flow Direction is any of [R2L or L2R or R2R]
AND when the flow matches X509 Certificate Public Key Algorithm is not N/A, X509 Certificate Public Key Size is not N/A

AND when the flow matches ("x509 certificate public key size" < 2048 AND "x509 certificate public key algorithm" != 'id-ecPublicKey') OR ("x509 certificate public key size" < 256 AND "x509 certificate public key algorithm" = 'id-ecPublicKey') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - Weak Public Key Length
Event description: Detected when an SSL/TLS session has a low public key bit count.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
